mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 200 (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 increases > 1% in 4mo and UNRATE decreases > 0.2pp in 1mo
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when both GDPC1 rises >1% and UNRATE falls >0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) during low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) indicates tight labor markets, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when PAYEMS rises >100k and UNRATE < 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side momentum, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when INDPRO rises >0.5% in 2mo.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when both HOUST and RSAFS rise above their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 5% signals restrictive monetary policy, which suppresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) paired with stable unemployment (UNRATE change < 0.1pp) indicates sustainable labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative when PAYEMS rises >100k and UNRATE change < 0.1pp.
